title: storage-out-of-tree-refs: kill HEAD drift by moving issues off the bookmark to refs/jjf/*
body:
# Goal

Move jjforge's storage from "files on the `issues` bookmark"
to out-of-tree refs (`refs/jjf/*`, mirroring git-bug's
`refs/bugs/*` pattern), so jjforge can be hosted in any
colocated jj+git repo without driving git HEAD onto an empty
descendant of the issues bookmark.

End state: `jjf init` and every mutating verb works against
the current git repo with no working-tree drift, no sibling
clone, no `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` bypass, no HEAD-recovery
dance.

# Why now

The host-asterinas migration (`cc2fa96`) tried to colocate
jj on `~/p/asterinas-workspace` (a repo with live source on
`main`) and call `jjf init`. The init succeeded but `jj`'s
4-CLI write dance moved git HEAD to `refs/jj/root` — wiping
the working tree from git's point of view. Recovery was
manual (`git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main && git reset
--hard main`) and lost the operator's uncommitted MEMORY.md
edits + a new amdgpu-rs-port directory. Documented in the
2026-06-23 session.

This is the SAME class of bug that motivated the
`JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` guard on jjforge's own source repo
(issue `08cf14b`). The guard prevents the drift on the
jjforge repo specifically; it doesn't help any OTHER
colocated repo that wants to host its own planner.

The asterinas migration is currently blocked on this. The
interim workaround for asterinas is a sibling working dir
(`git clone ~/p/asterinas-workspace ~/p/asterinas-workspace-data`,
init jjforge in the sibling, push/pull the `issues` bookmark
to share). Two checkouts on disk, forever. Cognitive cost on
every contributor.

git-bug solved this by storing all data in `refs/bugs/*` —
out-of-tree refs that live in the git object database but
never touch HEAD or the working tree. The current jjforge
choice ("files on a bookmark") is the root cause. This is a
v2 → v3 storage spec bump.

# Scope

This is a research ticket. Output is a design doc + a
follow-up implementation epic, not the implementation itself.
The design has to answer:

## Storage shape

- One ref per issue? (`refs/jjf/issues/<id>` → blob containing
  the JSON.) git-bug uses this for bugs.
- Single ref for the whole bookmark? (`refs/jjf/issues` → tree
  containing all issue files.)
- Tradeoff: per-issue refs scale (no whole-bookmark rewrite
  per op) but balloon ref count at 968 issues. Tree-shaped
  ref means every write rewrites a tree object, but ref count
  stays at 1.
- git-bug's per-bug-per-op chain (one ref per bug, each
  pointing at a chain of operation commits) is the strongest
  prior art. Worth a careful read of git-bug's `bug` package
  source.

## Op log

- Today: each mutating verb writes one git commit with a
  `Jjf-Op: <kind>` trailer, lands on the `issues` bookmark.
  Bookmark IS the op log (linear or merged from siblings).
- After redesign: where does the op log live? If per-issue
  refs, each ref's commit chain is that issue's op log. If
  whole-bookmark ref, append a parallel ref for ops.
- The trailer-shape parsing logic (just hardened in
  `1dce1ec`) is portable across either choice.

## Sync

- `jjf push` today is `git push origin issues:issues`. With
  refspec change: `git push origin 'refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*'`.
- Does this round-trip cleanly via standard git transport?
  (git-bug's experience: yes, but some hosts don't display
  non-`refs/heads/` / `refs/tags/` refs in their web UI;
  out-of-tree planners are invisible from the web view of
  the repo.)

## Migration from v2

- v2 → v3 needs a one-shot migrator: walk the existing
  `issues` bookmark's history, replay every op onto the new
  refs. Or: just replay the latest snapshot of each issue
  and lose the op-log granularity (probably fine; the trailer
  log was never queryable from `jjf`-land anyway).
- Detection: same shape as the v1 → v2 auto-migration. First
  `Storage::open` on a v2-shape repo detects and migrates.

## What gets removed

- `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` env-var + the preflight refusal in
  every mutating verb (CLAUDE.md "Operating in a colocated
  jj+git repo" section, issue `08cf14b`). All of it dies.
- The sibling-working-dir pattern for jjforge's own repo
  becomes unnecessary.

## What stays

- The on-disk JSON shape per issue (we're just changing where
  it's stored, not the schema).
- The CLI surface (all verbs work the same).
- The `jjf push`/`pull` semantics from the operator's view.
- The trailer-shape op log machinery — useful as the commit
  shape on the per-issue ref chains.

# Acceptance

- [ ] Design doc lands at `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` (or
  similar): chosen storage shape, op log shape, sync refspec,
  migration plan from v2.
- [ ] Implementation epic filed with sketched child tickets.
- [ ] Cost estimate (rough): how many sessions of work, and
  whether to do it before or after `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate`.
- [ ] Decision on whether to ship the asterinas migration
  via the sibling-clone interim or wait for the redesign.

# Out of scope

- The implementation itself. This ticket produces a design;
  the next epic implements it.
- Changing the trailer-shape parser (it's portable as-is).
- Changing the CLI surface.
- Changing the on-disk JSON schema (separate concern; the
  storage layer's input is the same).

# Pointers

- jjforge today: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` (the
  4-CLI write dance), `crates/jjf-storage/src/trailer.rs`
  (op log shape), `docs/storage-format.md` (v2 spec).
- git-bug prior art: archived git-bug source under
  `refs/bugs/*`. Specifically the `bug` package's storage
  layer; `bug new`/`bug comment`/`bug edit` round-trip via
  git-only-refs.
- HEAD-drift problem: `08cf14b` (the original guard on
  jjforge's own repo) and the 2026-06-23 asterinas
  recovery session.
- Asterinas migration ticket: `cc2fa96` (blocked on the
  outcome of this research).

# Why this is a research ticket, not a feature ticket

Three storage shapes are viable (per-issue refs / whole-bookmark
ref / git-bug-style commit-chain per issue) and the choice
binds the rest of the implementation. The research output
locks the choice; implementation is a separate epic. Filing
as feature would conflate the design call with the work.
